US: The United States declared independence on July 4, 1776, from British rule.
The US is home to the world's largest economy with a GDP of over $25 trillion.
The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France to commemorate Franco-American alliance in 1886.
GB: The United Kingdom consists of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.
Big Ben, officially called the Great Bell, is located in the Palace of Westminster.
London is the capital and one of the oldest major settlements in the world, founded by Romans.
FR: France is the most visited country in the world, with over 90 million international visitors annually.
The Eiffel Tower was built for the 1889 World's Fair and was originally intended to be temporary.
France is home to the Louvre Museum, the world's most visited art museum with the Mona Lisa.
DE: Germany is located in central Europe and shares borders with 9 different countries.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War and reunification of Germany.
Germany is the largest economy in Europe and a global leader in manufacturing and engineering.
JP: Japan is an island nation in East Asia composed of four main islands: Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.
Tokyo is the capital and world's largest metropolitan area with over 37 million residents.
Japan has the world's fastest trains - the Shinkansen bullet trains travel at speeds up to 320 km/h.
IN: India is the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion people and 22 official languages.
The Taj Mahal is one of the Seven Wonders of the World, built as a mausoleum by Emperor Shah Jahan.
India's Bollywood film industry produces the most movies globally, more than Hollywood or any other industry.
BR: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth largest country in the world.
The Amazon Rainforest, located primarily in Brazil, produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
Brazil hosted the 2014 FIFA World Cup and the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro.
AU: Australia is the smallest continent and the largest island in the world.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system and spans over 2,300 kilometers.
Australia has unique wildlife found nowhere else on Earth, including kangaroos, koalas, and platypuses.
CA: Canada is the second-largest country by land area, spanning six time zones across North America.
Niagara Falls, located on the border with the USA, is one of the most powerful waterfalls in the world.
Canada is a bilingual country with English and French as official languages since 1969.
MX: Mexico is home to the ancient Aztec civilization and Tenochtitlan, one of the world's largest cities in the 1500s.
The Mesoamerican Barrier Reef System is the world's second-largest reef system after the Great Barrier Reef.
Mexico City is built on the site of the ancient Aztec capital and is one of the world's largest metropolitan areas.
